metadata.etymology: Pictograph of a single horizontal stroke representing the number one
metadata.notes: The first radical in the Kangxi system. Also a character meaning 'one'.

metadata.etymology: Pictograph of a vertical line or rod
metadata.notes: Represents a vertical line. Rarely used alone but common as a component.

metadata.etymology: Pictograph of a dot or mark
metadata.notes: Represents a simple dot stroke. Found in many characters as a component.

metadata.etymology: Derived from 水 (shuǐ) — water
metadata.notes: One of the most common radicals. Appears on the left side of characters.

metadata.etymology: Derived from 人 (rén) — person. The left-side form of the 'person' radical.
metadata.notes: Common left-side form of the person radical. Appears in characters related to people.

metadata.etymology: Derived from 刀 (dāo) — knife/sword. The right-side form of the 'knife' radical.
metadata.notes: Common right-side form. Appears in characters involving cutting or separating.

metadata.etymology: Pictograph of a muscular arm showing strength
metadata.notes: Also a standalone character meaning 'strength' or 'power'. Found in characters related to effort.

metadata.etymology: Pictograph of a white rice grain
Pictograph of a right hand reaching — extended to mean 'again' or 'also'
metadata.notes: Also a standalone character meaning 'again'. Found in characters related to hands or repetition.

metadata.etymology: Pictograph of an open mouth
metadata.notes: One of the most common radicals. Found in hundreds of characters related to speech, eating, and sound.

metadata.etymology: Pictograph of a mound of earth or soil
metadata.notes: Also a standalone character meaning 'earth' or 'soil'. Found in characters related to ground and locations.

metadata.etymology: Pictograph of a foot or leg walking — extended to mean 'go' or 'arrive'
metadata.notes: Resembles 夊 (suī) but with different stroke placement. Found in characters related to movement.

metadata.etymology: Pictograph of a person standing with arms outstretched — implying 'big' or 'great'
metadata.notes: Also a common standalone character meaning 'big' or 'large'. Found in many compound characters.

metadata.etymology: Pictograph of a woman kneeling with folded hands
metadata.notes: Also a standalone character meaning 'woman' or 'female'. Found in characters related to gender and family.

metadata.etymology: Pictograph of a roof or shelter covering
metadata.notes: Always appears as the top component of characters. Found in characters related to buildings and homes.

metadata.etymology: Pictograph of a hand at the wrist — indicating the inch measurement from the pulse point
metadata.notes: Also a standalone character and unit of measurement. Found in characters related to measurement and time.

metadata.etymology: Pictograph of a heart
Derived from 手 (shǒu) — hand. The left-side form of the 'hand' radical.
metadata.notes: Extremely common left-side radical. Appears in hundreds of characters related to hand actions.

metadata.etymology: Pictograph of flames rising upward
metadata.notes: Also a standalone character meaning 'fire'. Bottom form 灬 appears in characters related to heat and cooking.

metadata.etymology: Derived from 玉 (yù) — jade. The side form of the 'jade' radical, identical in appearance to 王 (king).
metadata.notes: Visually identical to 王 (wáng — king) but used as the jade radical. Found in characters related to gems and precious objects.
